salientSummary: Added 3 new GitError cases (remoteRejected, hookRejected, protectedBranch) plus ErrorClassifier stderr patterns + 6 new Swift Testing cases. Specific patterns (protectedBranch, hookRejected) are checked before the generic remoteRejected, and all three are checked before the local-side nonFastForward hint so that server-side 'denying non-fast-forward' surfaces as remoteRejected. Unblocks M5-pull-push toasts.
whatWasImplemented: Sources/Git/GitError.swift: 3 new cases with LocalizedError strings shaped for toast rendering. Sources/Git/ErrorClassifier.swift: pattern block added and placed BEFORE the non-fast-forward check. Three private extractor helpers (hookDeclinedReason, protectedBranchDetail, remoteRejectedReason) pull an actionable detail line from multi-line stderr (e.g. the 'Changes must be made through a pull request.' follow-up after GH006, or the suffix of 'hook declined: <reason>'). Tests/KiteTests/GitTests/ErrorClassifierTests.swift: 3 fixtures added to the parameterised suite (hookRejected, protectedBranch, remoteRejected), 3 detail-extraction tests, and 2 precedence tests (hookRejected/protectedBranch beat the bare [remote rejected] footer). ClassifierKind enum gained 3 kinds.
whatWasLeftUndone: None for this fix feature. Toast rendering of the new cases is owned by M5-pull-push.

commandsRun.observation: Test run with 169 tests in 24 suites passed after 57.488s. ErrorClassifier suite: 10 tests passed, parameterised case now runs 11 argument fixtures.

discoveredIssues.description: Previously, a server-side 'remote: error: denying non-fast-forward' would classify as GitError.nonFastForward because the non-fast-forward check came before any push-rejection check. After this feature, push-rejection patterns (protectedBranch, hookRejected, remoteRejected) run first, so server-denial messages are attributed to the remote rather than to the user's local branch state. Behaviour change is intentional and consistent with the spec for M1-fix-git-error-push-cases; the existing nonFastForward fixture still classifies correctly because it uses '[rejected]' not '[remote rejected]' and has no 'denying' text.
